Here is an example of a PlantUML diagram with the following description:
```
<!-- Plant UML diagram -->
<label id="plant_symbol">Plant Symbol</label>
<label id="symbol">Symbol</label>
<text id="value">Value</text>
<input id="input_value" type="Text" />
<button id="btn_activate">Activate Symbol</button>
```

Here's an example of a Code with the following description:
```
<!-- Plant UML diagram ...
                    
                    The following PlantUML code has syntax errors:
                    Here is an example of a PlantUML code that also contains invalid SyntaxError messages:

    <!-- Plant UML diagram -->
    <label id="plant_symbol">Plant Symbol</label>
    <label id="symbol">Symbol</label>
    <text id="value">Value</text>
    <input id="input_value" type="Text" />
```
In this example, the code has syntax errors in the following tags:

	* @startuml (to indicate that the code is syntactically incorrect) --> PlantUML diagram with invalid SyntaxError messages

    <!-- Plant UML diagram -->
    <label id="plant_symbol">Plant Symbol</label>
    <label id="symbol">Symbol</label>
    <text id="value">Value</text>
    <input id="input_value" type="Text" />
```
To fix the code, you would:

1. Use the `@startuml` or `@enduml` tags to indicate that the code is syntactically incorrect.
2. Replace the @startuml and @enduml tags with actual syntax errors in both tags.
3. Remove the @startuml and @enduml tags from the code.
4. Use a `@input_value` tag to indicate that the code is syntactically incorrect (corrected by replacing the @startuml and @enduml tags).
5. Add a `@btn_activate` tag to indicate that the code will be automatically activated with the following code:

    <!-- Plant UML diagram -->
    <label id="plant_symbol">Plant Symbol</label>
    <label id="symbol">Symbol</label>
    <text id="value">Value</text>
```
